overview.location: Northeast Portland, Oregon — 12 miles northeast of downtown Portland, just south of the Columbia River in Multnomah County
overview.passengerVolume: ~20 million passengers annually (2025, recovering to pre-pandemic levels)
overview.cargoVolume: Largest cargo airport in Oregon — handles over 95% of the state's air cargo
overview.distinction: Consistently rated the best airport in the United States. Named best U.S. airport by Travel + Leisure for seven consecutive years. Ranked #1 in J.D. Power customer satisfaction three times (most recently 2019) and top 5 every year from 2015 to 2020. Famous for its iconic teal carpet, which became a global pop culture phenomenon with its own Instagram hashtag (#pdxcarpet) and Wikipedia page. Alaska Airlines' second-largest hub. Accounts for 90% of Oregon's passenger air travel.
overview.dominantAirline: Alaska Airlines (largest carrier — ~34% of passenger traffic, operates primarily from Concourses B and C)
overview.founded: 1940 (as Portland-Columbia Airport; renamed Portland International Airport in 1957)
overview.metroAccess: TriMet MAX Red Line light rail station directly at the terminal — $2.80 fare to downtown Portland, ~40 minutes
overview.militaryUse: Oregon Air National Guard 142nd Wing (F-15 Eagles) operates from adjacent Portland Air National Guard Base
overview.internationalNote: PDX offers nonstop international service to Canada (Vancouver, Calgary, Toronto), Mexico (Guadalajara, Puerto Vallarta, Cabo San Lucas), Japan (Tokyo-Narita via Delta), Netherlands (Amsterdam via Delta seasonal), and the UK (London via Delta seasonal). Iceland (Reykjavik via Icelandair seasonal).

terminals.name: Concourse B
terminals.notes: Alaska Airlines and Horizon Air primary concourse. JetBlue and American Airlines also operate here. Accessible via the south security checkpoint. Connected to Concourses D and E via the concourse connector.

terminals.name: Concourse C
terminals.status: Active — 18 gates (C5-C13, C15-C23). Largest concourse. South security checkpoint.
terminals.notes: The largest concourse at PDX with 18 gates. Serves Alaska Airlines overflow plus most low-cost and ultra-low-cost carriers. Pet relief area near gates C1/C2. Accessible via south security checkpoint.

terminals.name: Concourse D
terminals.notes: Delta Air Lines' primary concourse at PDX. Handles Delta's domestic and international flights (Tokyo-Narita, Amsterdam, London seasonal). Delta Sky Club between gates D5-D7. Escape Lounge near gate D8 (Priority Pass). International arrivals. North security checkpoint open 24/7.

terminals.name: Concourse E
terminals.notes: Extended in 2020 with 6 additional gates for Southwest Airlines' relocation from Concourse C. United Airlines also operates here. Accessible via north security checkpoint or concourse connector.

transit: TriMet MAX Red Line (direct light rail to downtown Portland, Beaverton, Hillsboro — $2.80 fare, ~40 min to downtown)
Amtrak Cascades and Coast Starlight (Portland Union Station, accessible via MAX)

maxLightRail.description: TriMet MAX Red Line light rail connects PDX directly to downtown Portland, Beaverton, and Hillsboro. Station located at the west end of the terminal building near baggage claim — no shuttle required.
maxLightRail.fare: $2.80 single trip (adult). $1.40 youth/honored citizen. $5.50 day pass. Contactless payment accepted.
maxLightRail.frequency: Every 15 minutes during peak hours, every 20-30 minutes evenings/weekends
maxLightRail.hours: First train arrives at PDX at 4:43 AM, last train departs PDX at 12:27 AM
maxLightRail.accessFromTerminal: Walk directly from baggage claim to the MAX station at the west end of the terminal — no shuttle needed
maxLightRail.competitorNote: At $2.80, the MAX Red Line is the cheapest and most reliable way from PDX to downtown Portland. The 38-minute travel time is competitive with driving during rush hour and significantly cheaper than the $30-50 taxi fare. No shuttle, no transfer — walk directly from the terminal to the train.

taxi.pickup: Transportation Plaza, Level 1 of Short-Term Parking Garage (follow signs from baggage claim)
taxi.note: Taxis pick up at the Transportation Plaza inside Level 1 of the Short-Term Parking Garage. Follow signs from baggage claim. Cash and credit cards accepted.

rideshare.pickupLocation: Transportation Plaza, Level 1 of the Short-Term Parking Garage. Each garage column is labeled with the pick-up spot for Uber, Lyft, or Wingz. Follow signs from baggage claim.
rideshare.surgePricing: Surge pricing common during Rose Festival, Trail Blazers games, Timbers/Thorns matches, holiday weekends, and major snowstorms
rideshare.note: Rideshare is usually cheaper than taxis from PDX. The new Transportation Plaza (opened with the terminal renovation) consolidates all rideshare and taxi pickup in one convenient location inside the parking garage.

rentalCars.location: Rental Car Center adjacent to the terminal — accessible via a short covered walkway from the terminal building
rentalCars.note: All rental car companies are consolidated at the Rental Car Center adjacent to the terminal. Short covered walkway from the terminal — no shuttle needed. Return your car at the same location.

groundTransportation.hotelShuttles: Many nearby hotels along NE Airport Way and NE 82nd Avenue offer complimentary airport shuttle service. Check with your hotel before booking. Popular shuttle hotels include Holiday Inn, Hampton Inn, Courtyard by Marriott, and Sheraton.

parkingRates: On-airport: Short-Term Garage $3/hr ($27/day), Long-Term Garage $24/day, Economy Red/Blue Lots $12/day (7th day free), Gold Key Valet $10/hr ($35/day). Off-site: from ~$6.45/day with shuttle.

shortTermGarage.name: Short-Term Parking Garage
shortTermGarage.serves: All concourses — directly connected to terminal
shortTermGarage.features: Closest parking to the terminal. Direct covered walkway to check-in and baggage claim. Transportation Plaza (rideshare/taxi pickup) on Level 1. Best for pickups, drop-offs, and short stays.

longTermGarage.name: Long-Term Parking Garage
longTermGarage.serves: All concourses — walking distance through Short-Term Garage
longTermGarage.features: Located adjacent to the Short-Term Garage with covered walkway to terminal. Best on-airport option for multi-day trips. No shuttle needed — walk to terminal in ~5-7 minutes.

economyParking.name: Economy Parking (Red and Blue Lots)
economyParking.features: Best budget option for on-airport parking. Two lots (Red and Blue) located near I-205. 7th day free makes it ideal for week-long trips ($72 for 7 days). Disabled parking near bus shelters in both lots.
economyParking.note: Economy lots can fill up during peak travel periods (summer, holidays). Arrive early or pre-book. The 7th-day-free promotion is a significant savings for weekly travelers.

valetParking.name: Gold Key Valet Parking
valetParking.features: Premium drop-off at the terminal curb. Car stored in covered garage. Vehicle delivered to you upon return. Most convenient option for frequent flyers.

cellPhoneLot.name: Cell Phone Waiting Lot
cellPhoneLot.location: NE Airport Way and NE 82nd Avenue
cellPhoneLot.rules: For passenger pickup only. Driver must remain with vehicle. Maximum 30-minute stay.

locations: Short-Term Garage Level 4 (6 Level 2 charging ports, 3 dual-connector stations)
Economy Red Lot (12 Level 1 PowerPost stations)
Economy Blue Lot (12 Level 1 PowerPost stations)
evCharging.note: PDX has 48 EV charging stations — one of the largest airport EV charging installations in the U.S. Level 2 chargers in the Short-Term Garage provide faster charging. Economy lot chargers are Level 1 (slower, best for multi-day trips).

onAirportParking.accessibleParking: Accessible parking available in all parking areas. Economy lots have disabled spaces adjacent to shuttle bus shelters. Short-Term and Long-Term garages have accessible spaces near elevators.
onAirportParking.paymentMethods: Cash, Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, contactless payment
onAirportParking.garageServiceAreas: Short-Term Garage → direct terminal access. Long-Term Garage → covered walkway to terminal. Economy Lots → free shuttle every 7-9 minutes.

parkingLots.name: Jiffy Portland Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free 24/7 on-demand shuttle to and from PDX terminal, approximately every 10-15 minutes
parkingLots.overview: Popular off-site lot 1.4 miles from PDX with 24/7 on-demand shuttle service. Fenced and gated facility with security cameras. One of the most established off-site options near PDX.
parkingLots.notes: Well-established PDX off-site lot. 24/7 shuttle is reliable. Gated and fenced lot with security cameras. Pre-booking online saves ~$1.50/day.

parkingLots.name: AirPark Portland Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free on-demand shuttle 24 hours a day — driver picks you up at your car and takes you directly to the terminal
parkingLots.overview: Off-site parking 1.2 miles from PDX. Drivers are picked up at their car and shuttled directly to the terminal. One of the closest off-site options.
parkingLots.notes: Closest off-site lot to PDX. Personal pickup at your car is a nice touch. Pre-booking saves ~$3/day vs walk-up rate.

parkingLots.name: Park Shuttle and Fly (PDX)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle every 15 minutes to PDX terminal
parkingLots.overview: Off-site parking at 6455 NE 82nd Avenue with regular shuttle service to PDX. Gated lot with 24/7 security and surveillance.
parkingLots.notes: Regular shuttle every 15 minutes. Gated and secure. Convenient location on NE 82nd Avenue near airport.

parkingLots.name: Holiday Inn Portland Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free airport shuttle service during operating hours
parkingLots.overview: Hotel parking near PDX with competitive rates. No hotel stay required for parking. One of the cheapest pre-book rates near PDX at $6.45/day.
parkingLots.notes: Best budget off-site option when pre-booked at $6.45/day. Hotel lot is well-lit and monitored. Verify shuttle hours for early/late flights.

parkingLots.name: Super 8 PDX Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free airport shuttle — check with hotel for schedule
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly hotel parking near PDX. Park-sleep-fly packages also available for travelers with early morning flights.
parkingLots.notes: Good budget option. Park-sleep-fly packages available if you need a pre-flight hotel stay. Verify shuttle schedule before booking.

parkingLots.name: Hampton Inn & Suites Portland Airport Parking
parkingLots.overview: Quality hotel parking option near PDX with reliable shuttle service. No hotel stay required. Well-maintained lot with good lighting.
parkingLots.notes: Reliable shuttle and well-maintained lot. Hampton Inn quality. Good option for travelers who value a trustworthy brand.

shuttleInfo: On-airport: Economy Parking (Red and Blue Lots) has free 24/7 shuttle every 7-9 minutes. Short-Term and Long-Term garages require no shuttle (direct walk to terminal). Off-site: Most lots include free shuttle — Jiffy and AirPark offer 24/7 on-demand service. Hotel lots may have limited shuttle hours — always verify before booking for early/late flights.
distanceFromCity: 12 miles northeast of downtown Portland. 20-40 minutes by car depending on traffic. ~38 minutes by MAX Red Line ($2.80). Portland's only commercial airport — handles 90% of Oregon's passenger air travel.

keyDistances.toDowntownPortland: 12 miles, 20-40 min by car, 38 min by MAX Red Line ($2.80), $20-35 rideshare
keyDistances.toLloydDistrict: 8 miles, 15-25 min by car, 25 min by MAX, $15-25 rideshare
keyDistances.toPearlDistrict: 12 miles, 20-40 min by car, 40 min by MAX, $20-35 rideshare
keyDistances.toBeaverton: 20 miles, 25-40 min by car, 65 min by MAX, $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toSeattle: 175 miles, 3-3.5 hours by car, ~3.5 hours by Amtrak Cascades
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: PDX's MAX Red Line provides cheap ($2.80) direct light rail to downtown Portland in ~38 minutes — no shuttle, no transfer. The station is built directly into the terminal. For travelers staying downtown, the MAX eliminates the need for parking or rideshare entirely. PDX is also uniquely compact — one terminal, four concourses — so ground transportation connections are simpler than at multi-terminal airports.

construction2026.status: PDX Next — $2 billion terminal redevelopment program nearing completion. Phase 1 (new main terminal) opened August 2024. Phase 2 completing in 2026.
projects: New Main Terminal (Phase 1, completed August 2024) — Pacific Northwest-inspired architecture with mass timber ceiling, 9-acre large-span roof, doubled ticketing/lobby area, new pre- and post-security concessions featuring local Portland businesses.
Terminal Expansion Phase 2 (completing early-mid 2026) — North and south expansion of post-security spaces. New concessions, seating areas, and passenger amenities.
Concourse Connector Restoration — Walkway connecting Concourses B/C to D/E reopened after temporary construction closure.
New Alaska Lounge (opening 2026) — 13,000+ sq ft lounge with Pacific Northwest design, fireplace, barista station, double the seating of the temporary Express Lounge.
150-foot Multi-Floor Addition — New structure between Concourses C and D with additional amenities.
Local Business Concessions — Portland-area restaurants and shops integrated throughout the new terminal (e.g., Stumptown Coffee, Portland food scene represented).
construction2026.travelImpact: Most construction is complete or in final phases. Walking distances between Concourses B/E were temporarily longer during construction but normalizing as Phase 2 wraps up. No parking closures. The new terminal is fully operational.

tsaPreCheck.hours: TSA PreCheck lanes typically open 4:00 AM - 4:00 PM daily
tsaPreCheck.note: TSA PreCheck available at both security checkpoints

clear.note: CLEAR Plus uses biometric identity verification to expedite the screening process. Can be combined with TSA PreCheck.

globalEntry.note: Global Entry kiosks available in the arrivals area for international passengers.

standardWaitTimes.peakWait: Up to 45 minutes during peak hours
standardWaitTimes.tips: PDX is a compact airport — security lines are generally reasonable. Early morning (5-8 AM) is the busiest. TSA PreCheck significantly reduces wait times.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours during summer peak, Thanksgiving, Christmas, spring break

concourseD.name: Delta Sky Club
concourseD.location: Concourse D, between gates D5 and D7
concourseD.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta One, Delta Reserve Amex, SkyTeam Elite Plus
concourseD.amenities: Complimentary food and beverages, full-service bar, local craft beer, WiFi, workspaces, runway views.

concourseD.name: Escape Lounge
concourseD.access: Priority Pass, American Express Platinum (through Priority Pass), day pass purchase
concourseD.amenities: Freshly prepared food, cocktails, local wines and craft beer, WiFi, power outlets, runway views. Day passes available.

concourseBC.name: Alaska Express Lounge (temporary)
concourseBC.access: Alaska Airlines Lounge membership, First Class on qualifying flights, day pass ($65)
concourseBC.amenities: Temporary lounge accommodating up to 35 guests. Snacks, beverages, WiFi. Will be replaced by the new 13,000+ sq ft Alaska Lounge opening 2026.

upcoming.name: Alaska Lounge (new, opening 2026)
upcoming.access: Alaska Airlines Lounge membership, First Class, day pass ($65)
upcoming.amenities: 13,000+ sq ft. Fireplace, Pacific Northwest design, custom signature Loungers, barista station with hand-crafted espresso, local craft beer and West Coast wines, complimentary food, spectacular terminal views. Double the seating of the temporary Express Lounge.

wifi.note: Connect and accept Terms and Conditions. No time limit.

economyLots.location: Economy Red Lot (12 stations) and Economy Blue Lot (12 stations)

evCharging.totalPorts: 48 EV charging ports (one of the largest airport installations in the U.S.)
evCharging.note: PDX was a pioneer in airport EV charging — 48 stations across parking areas. Level 2 chargers in Short-Term Garage charge faster; Level 1 PowerPosts in Economy lots are best for multi-day trips where slow charging is sufficient.

luggageStorage.onSite: Luggage storage is NOT available on-site at PDX for security reasons.

offSiteAlternatives.locations: Downtown Portland, Pearl District, Lloyd District

locations.name: Landside Pet Relief Area
locations.features: Artificial grass, fire hydrant, refuse bags, sink, bench, ADA/wheelchair accessible, luggage counter

locations.name: Airside Pet Relief Area
locations.features: Artificial grass, decorative rock, refuse bags, sink, bench, ADA/wheelchair accessible, luggage counter

petFacilities.note: Both pet relief areas are ADA accessible. Animals must remain on a leash at all times.

peakTravelTimes.seasonalPeak: June through September is peak season (summer travel to the Pacific Northwest). Thanksgiving week, Christmas/New Year, and spring break (March-April) are also heavy. Rose Festival (June) brings additional regional traffic. Ski season (December-March) increases weekend traffic for Mt. Hood travelers.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours during summer peak and holiday weekends

weatherPatterns.climate: Marine west coast climate. Mild, wet winters and warm, dry summers. Portland averages 36 inches of rain per year, mostly October through May.
weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Ice storms are the primary severe disruption risk — freezing rain from cold air funneling through the Columbia River Gorge can shut down PDX for 1-3 days. Winter fog can cause delays and low-visibility approaches. Snow is rare but paralyzing when it occurs (Portland lacks major snow removal infrastructure).
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: June through September — dry, warm, clear skies. July and August are peak with minimal precipitation and temperatures around 80°F.
weatherPatterns.worstMonths: December through February (ice storm risk + persistent rain + occasional snow events)
weatherPatterns.iceStormNote: PDX's biggest weather threat is ice storms caused by cold east winds channeling through the Columbia River Gorge meeting moist Pacific air. These freezing rain events can completely shut down the airport and roads for 1-3 days. Major ice storms occurred in January 2024 and February 2025. Travel insurance strongly recommended for December-February travel.
weatherPatterns.winterFog: Dense fog is common October-February, especially in the Willamette Valley. Can cause ground delays and diversions. Morning fog usually burns off by midday.
weatherPatterns.rainNote: Portland's reputation for rain is earned — October through May is consistently wet. However, rain rarely causes flight cancellations. Drizzle and overcast skies are the norm, not heavy downpours. Summer (June-September) is remarkably dry and sunny.
weatherPatterns.note: Morning flights are the most reliable year-round. Winter travelers should monitor Columbia River Gorge wind conditions — east wind events signal potential freezing rain.

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near PDX. Several hotels along NE Airport Way and NE 82nd Avenue offer park-sleep-fly packages including 7-14 days of free parking.
parkSleepFly.priceRange: From ~$93/night (1-night stay + up to 14 days parking + free airport shuttle)

popularHotels.name: Holiday Inn Portland Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Cheapest pre-book rate. Good for budget-conscious travelers.

popularHotels.name: Four Points by Sheraton Portland Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Marriott brand reliability. Park-sleep-fly packages available.

holidayInn.caveat: Verify shuttle hours for early/late flights

maxRedLineRoundTrip.note: $2.80 per ride. Cheapest option for downtown Portland travelers. $5.50 day pass if doing multiple trips.

rideshareRoundTrip.note: Cheaper than parking for trips of any length if staying downtown

savingsComparison.keyDifference: PDX's Economy Lots at $12/day with the 7th day free ($72/week) are excellent value for on-airport parking. Off-site lots start at $6.45/day pre-booked. The MAX Red Line at $2.80 is unbeatable for downtown Portland travelers — direct walk from the terminal to the train, no shuttle needed. For longer trips, Economy parking or off-site lots save $100+ vs the Long-Term Garage.

uniqueFeatures.bestAirportReputation: PDX has been consistently ranked the best airport in the United States. Named best domestic airport by Travel + Leisure for seven consecutive years. Ranked #1 in J.D. Power's North America Airport Satisfaction Study three times. Known for its compact layout, friendly staff, local food and shopping, and stress-free travel experience.
uniqueFeatures.iconicCarpet: PDX's teal geometric carpet is one of the most famous airport features in the world. Designed by SRG Partnership in 1987, the pattern represents the airport's runways as seen by air traffic controllers at night. The carpet spawned a pop culture phenomenon with its own Instagram hashtag (#pdxcarpet, 20,000+ posts), Wikipedia page, and merchandise (socks, shirts, bike helmets). After a temporary replacement, the original carpet returned to the new terminal in 2024.
uniqueFeatures.directMaxConnection: PDX is one of the few major U.S. airports with a light rail station built directly into the terminal — no shuttle, no bus, no transfer. Walk from baggage claim to the MAX Red Line in minutes. $2.80 to downtown Portland.
uniqueFeatures.localFoodAndShopping: PDX bucks the typical airport food trend by featuring beloved local Portland restaurants and shops. The terminal showcases the city's food culture with local eateries, craft beer, and artisan products — at street-level pricing (PDX was one of the first airports to mandate street pricing).
uniqueFeatures.streetPricing: PDX pioneered the 'street pricing' concept — airport restaurants and shops are required to charge the same prices as their downtown locations. No airport markup. This policy has been widely praised and adopted by other airports.
uniqueFeatures.pacificNorthwestArchitecture: The new terminal (opened August 2024) features stunning Pacific Northwest-inspired architecture by ZGF Architects with mass timber ceiling elements, natural light, and sustainable design. The 9-acre large-span roof is an engineering marvel.
uniqueFeatures.evChargingPioneer: With 48 EV charging stations, PDX was one of the first U.S. airports to install large-scale commercial EV charging infrastructure — reflecting Oregon's commitment to sustainable transportation.
uniqueFeatures.compactDesign: PDX's single-terminal, four-concourse layout makes it one of the most navigable major airports in the U.S. Maximum gate-to-gate walking time is about 15 minutes. No inter-terminal trains or buses needed.
uniqueFeatures.oregonAirNationalGuard: The Oregon Air National Guard 142nd Wing operates F-15 Eagles from the adjacent Portland Air National Guard Base — travelers may spot military jets alongside commercial aircraft.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs for article generation. Verified 2026-04-09.
